mission.title: Install and Configure vLLM
mission.description: vLLM is a high-throughput and memory-efficient inference and serving engine for large language models (LLMs). It is ideal for applications requiring fast and scalable LLM serving. Key trade-offs include the need for GPU resources for optimal performance and the complexity of configuration for advanced features.

steps.title: Add Helm repository
steps.description:
This step adds the vLLM Helm chart repository to your local Helm configuration.

```bash

helm repo add vllm https://github.com/vllm-project/vllm/releases/download/v0.18.0

```

> This command sets up the repository for the latest stable release.

steps.title: Install Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs)
steps.description:
CRDs are necessary for the vLLM operator to function correctly within your Kubernetes cluster.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/vllm-project/vllm/v0.18.0/deploy/crds/vllm.io_vllms_crd.yaml

```

> This command installs the required CRDs.

steps.title: Install vLLM using Helm
steps.description:
This command installs the vLLM operator in the specified namespace, allowing you to manage LLM deployments.

```bash

helm install vllm vllm/vllm --namespace vllm --create-namespace --version 0.18.0

```

> This installs the vLLM operator with the specified version.

steps.title: Create an example Custom Resource (CR)
steps.description:
This step creates an example vLLM instance to demonstrate how to deploy a model.

```bash

kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: vllm.io/v1
kind: VLLM
metadata:
  name: example-vllm
spec:
  model: 'gpt-3'
EOF

```

> This creates a vLLM instance using a sample model.
commands:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: vllm.io/v1
kind: VLLM
metadata:
  name: example-vllm
spec:
  model: 'gpt-3'
EOF

steps.title: Verify the vLLM instance
steps.description:
This command checks the status of the vLLM instance to ensure it is running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get vllms -n vllm

```

> You should see the status of your vLLM instance.

uninstall.title: Uninstall vLLM
uninstall.description:
This step will remove the vLLM operator and all associated resources. Be aware that this may lead to data loss if any models were deployed.

```bash

helm uninstall vllm --namespace vllm

kubectl delete namespace vllm

```

> This command uninstalls the vLLM operator.
> This command deletes the namespace and all resources within it.

upgrade.title: Upgrade vLLM
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you have a backup of your configurations and data. This process involves draining any running instances, upgrading the Helm release, and verifying the upgrade.

```bash

kubectl drain <node-name> --ignore-daemonsets --delete-local-data

helm upgrade vllm vllm/vllm --namespace vllm --version 0.18.0

kubectl get vllms -n vllm

```

> Replace <node-name> with the name of your node to drain.
> This command upgrades vLLM to the specified version.
> Verify that the vLLM instance is running correctly after the upgrade.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'vllm.io/v1' not found
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The CRDs may not have been installed correctly.

**Fix:**
Reapply the CRDs using the command: kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/vllm-project/vllm/v0.18.0/deploy/crds/vllm.io_vllms_crd.yaml

troubleshooting.title: vLLM instance is not starting
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** Insufficient resources allocated to the Kubernetes cluster.

**Fix:**
Ensure your cluster meets the resource requirements: Minimum 2 vCPU, 4GB RAM per node.

troubleshooting.title: kubectl get vllms shows 'Pending' status
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** There may be insufficient nodes or resources to schedule the vLLM pod.

**Fix:**
Check your node status and resource allocation: kubectl get nodes

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to pull image'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The specified container image may not be available or accessible.

**Fix:**
Verify the image name and ensure your Kubernetes cluster has access to the image repository.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new model types and improved memory management features.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps required for this version.

resolution.summary: You should see the vLLM instance listed with a status of 'Running' when executing: kubectl get vllms -n vllm.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster set up with the required resources and the necessary tools installed.
